key_drivers: The dollar remains a broad cross-asset tailwind: TradingView showed DXY near 98.23, still down on both five-day and one-month horizons despite a small daily bounce.
Risk and duration conditions are constructive but not euphoric because VIX stayed balanced in the high teens while VIX direction fell, MOVE fell on the week, and SOX extended its leadership.
Crude retains the firmest commodity setup because the latest official EIA week still points to a crude draw and a tightening supply backdrop.
FX leadership is driven by policy divergence: the Fed is best treated as a hawkish hold, while the ECB remains firm, the RBA stays hawkish, and the BoJ remains in a normalization regime.

stale_sources: Atlanta Fed GDPNow remains the April 9, 2026 official update at 1.3% for 2026:Q1.
FRED DFII10 and BAMLH0A0HYM2 latest official observations lag the report timestamp by normal publication delay.
World Gold Council ETF-flow evidence remains anchored to the latest monthly release.
Detailed CME FedWatch probabilities were only partly readable in-session, so Fed stance used visible probabilities plus conservative interpretation.
fallbacks_used: TradingView was used for DXY and SOX direction because those pages were easier to read directly than some alternative market pages.
Repository-verified same-week source snapshots were used to retain explicit MOVE, copper, China-growth, and gold-ETF classifications where direct live extraction was incomplete.
Repository-verified official central-bank references were used to preserve the March ECB, RBA, and BoJ stance classifications.
A readable same-session FedWatch view confirmed near-total pricing for no change at the next Fed meeting.
overnight_changes: DXY remained broadly weak over both five-day and one-month horizons.
VIX and MOVE both eased, improving the macro backdrop for duration-sensitive risk assets.
SOX and copper both strengthened, reinforcing pro-cyclical and commodity-currency leadership.

imminent: Atlanta Fed GDPNow update on April 21, 2026
near_term: BoJ normalization communication remains an active FX catalyst
background: Ongoing Gulf shipping and energy-supply headlines
